---
title: "Sample size requirements for contact surveys"
format: html
---

```{r setup}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(boot)
library(furrr)
library(patchwork)
setwd("/Users/eb15081/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UniversityofBristol/projects2022/samplesize/Rfiles/contact-sample-size/")

# Figure dimension constants (golden ratio proportions)
H <- 8
goldenratio <- (1 + sqrt(5)) / 2

# setting up parallel processing for furrr including number of cores used and 
# random seeding
no_cores <- availableCores() - 1
plan(multisession, workers = no_cores)

```



## POLYMOD sensitivity to age bands 

Sub-samples the POLYMOD dataset at a range of sample sizes, constructs a
contact matrix with 10-year age bands, and extracts the dominant eigenvalue
of each sampled matrix. Repeating this many times shows how stable the
dominant eigenvalue (a proxy for R0) is as a function of sample size.


```{r polymod-setup}
participantdata <- read.csv(
  "../3874557/2008_Mossong_POLYMOD_participant_common.csv"
)
contactdata <- read.csv(
  "../3874557/2008_Mossong_POLYMOD_contact_common.csv"
)


maxsize  <- nrow(participantdata)   # total participants in full dataset
x        <- c(100, 500, 1000, 1500, 2000, 3000, 4000, 5000)  # sample sizes
x2       <- seq(100, 5800, 100)
numreps  <- 200                     # repetitions per sample size

```


```{r polymod-simulation-10y}
#| cache: true   # simulation is slow; cache to avoid re-running on re-render

# Extract the per-draw computation into a plain function.
# Takes a sample size ss, runs the full sub-sampling pipeline once,
# and returns the scalar dominant eigenvalue of the contact matrix.

agebandsize=10

compute_eigenval_withreplacement <- function(ss) {
    ix = data.frame(part_id=sample(participantdata$part_id,size=ss,replace=TRUE))
    participantdata %>% 
      right_join(ix,multiple="all",unmatched="drop",by = join_by(part_id)) %>% #decide whether this participant is included in the sample, could be sampled more than once
      mutate(agegp1 = ifelse(part_age<=70,floor(part_age/agebandsize), floor(70/agebandsize))) %>%            #create age categories, in this case 10-year age bands
      add_count(agegp1) %>%  ##count partipants who are taking part
      right_join(contactdata %>%
                   filter(!is.na(cnt_age_exact)),relationship = "many-to-many",by = join_by(part_id)) %>% 
      mutate(agegp2 = ifelse(cnt_age_exact<=agebandsize,floor(cnt_age_exact/agebandsize),floor(70/agebandsize))) %>%#create age categories for contacts (make sure same as participant)
      group_by(agegp1,agegp2) %>%
      summarise(popsize=max(n),totalcontacts = n(),.groups = "drop_last") %>%
      mutate(contactrate = totalcontacts/popsize) %>%
      mutate(contactrate = ifelse(is.na(contactrate),0,contactrate)) %>% 
      pivot_wider(id_cols=agegp1,names_from=agegp2,values_from = contactrate, values_fill = 0) %>%   #turn into matrix
      replace_na(list(Value = 0))-> contactmatrix

    L1 <- ncol(contactmatrix)
    round(max(Re(eigen(contactmatrix[1:(L1 - 1), 2:(L1)])$values)), 3)
}



# Build a tidy grid of every (sample size × rep) combination and map the
# function over it. Each row is one independent draw; randomness comes from
# rbinom inside compute_eigenval, so the rep column is just a counter.
maxeigenval_v2 <- crossing(ss = x2, rep = seq_len(numreps)) %>%
  mutate(maxeigen = future_map_dbl(ss, compute_eigenval_withreplacement,
                                   .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE),
                                   .progress = TRUE))

#nconst=compute_eigenval_withreplacement(7000)
```


```{r polymod-normalise}

#norm_const <- mean(maxeigenval[length(x), ])
norm_const <- as.numeric(maxeigenval_v2 %>% filter(ss==max(ss)) %>% summarise(mean(maxeigen)))
#norm_const <-as.numeric(nconst)
#maxeigenval <- maxeigenval / norm_const
maxeigenval_v2 <- maxeigenval_v2 %>% mutate(maxeigen_norm = maxeigen / norm_const)
```

```{r savepolymodout}
#save(maxeigenval,file="maxeigenval.RData")
save(maxeigenval_v2,file="maxeigenval10_revision.RData") 
```


```{r polymod-scatter}
# Scatter of normalised dominant eigenvalues across all reps, by sample size

maxeigenval_v2 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ss, y = maxeigen_norm)) +
    geom_point(size = 1.2, alpha = 0.3, colour = 'darkblue') +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1,   colour = "black") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 2,   colour = "darkgrey", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, colour = "darkgrey", linetype = "dashed") +
    xlab("Sample size") +
    ylab(expression("Normalised " * R)) + 
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text  = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14)
    )-> paral10

paral10

ggsave("PM10.png", paral10, height = H, width = H * goldenratio,
       dpi = 600, device = "png")
```



```{r polymod-simulation-5y}
#| cache: true   # simulation is slow; cache to avoid re-running on re-render

# Extract the per-draw computation into a plain function.
# Takes a sample size ss, runs the full sub-sampling pipeline once,
# and returns the scalar dominant eigenvalue of the contact matrix.

agebandsize=5

compute_eigenval_withreplacement <- function(ss) {
    ix = data.frame(part_id=sample(participantdata$part_id,size=ss,replace=TRUE))
    participantdata %>% 
      right_join(ix,multiple="all",unmatched="drop",by = join_by(part_id)) %>% #decide whether this participant is included in the sample, could be sampled more than once
      mutate(agegp1 = ifelse(part_age<=70,floor(part_age/agebandsize), floor(70/agebandsize))) %>%            #create age categories, in this case 10-year age bands
      add_count(agegp1) %>%  ##count partipants who are taking part
      right_join(contactdata %>%
                   filter(!is.na(cnt_age_exact)),relationship = "many-to-many",by = join_by(part_id)) %>% 
      mutate(agegp2 = ifelse(cnt_age_exact<=agebandsize,floor(cnt_age_exact/agebandsize),floor(70/agebandsize))) %>%#create age categories for contacts (make sure same as participant)
      group_by(agegp1,agegp2) %>%
      summarise(popsize=max(n),totalcontacts = n(),.groups = "drop_last") %>%
      mutate(contactrate = totalcontacts/popsize) %>%
      mutate(contactrate = ifelse(is.na(contactrate),0,contactrate)) %>% 
      pivot_wider(id_cols=agegp1,names_from=agegp2,values_from = contactrate, values_fill = 0) %>%   #turn into matrix
      replace_na(list(Value = 0))-> contactmatrix

    L1 <- ncol(contactmatrix)
    round(max(Re(eigen(contactmatrix[1:(L1 - 1), 2:(L1)])$values)), 3)
}

# Build a tidy grid of every (sample size × rep) combination and map the
# function over it. Each row is one independent draw; randomness comes from
# rbinom inside compute_eigenval, so the rep column is just a counter.
maxeigenval_5y <- crossing(ss = x2, rep = seq_len(numreps)) %>%
  mutate(maxeigen = future_map_dbl(ss, compute_eigenval_withreplacement,
                                   .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE),
                                   .progress = TRUE))

#nconst=compute_eigenval_withreplacement(7000)
```


```{r}

#norm_const <- mean(maxeigenval[length(x), ])
norm_const <- as.numeric(maxeigenval_5y %>% filter(ss==max(ss)) %>% summarise(mean(maxeigen)))
#norm_const <-as.numeric(nconst)
#maxeigenval <- maxeigenval / norm_const
maxeigenval_5y <- maxeigenval_5y %>% mutate(maxeigen_norm = maxeigen / norm_const)
```

```{r}
#save(maxeigenval,file="maxeigenval.RData")
save(maxeigenval_5y,file="maxeigenval_5y.RData") 
```


```{r}
# Scatter of normalised dominant eigenvalues across all reps, by sample size

maxeigenval_5y %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ss, y = maxeigen_norm)) +
    geom_point(size = 1.2, alpha = 0.3, colour = 'darkblue') +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1,   colour = "black") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 2,   colour = "darkgrey", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, colour = "darkgrey", linetype = "dashed") +
    xlab("Sample size") +
    ylab(expression("Normalised " * R)) + 
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text  = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14)
    )-> paral5

paral5

ggsave("PM5.png", paral5, height = H, width = H * goldenratio,
       dpi = 600, device = "png")
```



```{r polymod-simulation-1y}
#| cache: true   # simulation is slow; cache to avoid re-running on re-render

# Extract the per-draw computation into a plain function.
# Takes a sample size ss, runs the full sub-sampling pipeline once,
# and returns the scalar dominant eigenvalue of the contact matrix.

agebandsize=1

compute_eigenval_withreplacement <- function(ss) {
    ix = data.frame(part_id=sample(participantdata$part_id,size=ss,replace=TRUE))
    participantdata %>% 
      right_join(ix,multiple="all",unmatched="drop",by = join_by(part_id)) %>% #decide whether this participant is included in the sample, could be sampled more than once
      mutate(agegp1 = ifelse(part_age<=70,floor(part_age/agebandsize), floor(70/agebandsize))) %>%            #create age categories, in this case 10-year age bands
      add_count(agegp1) %>%  ##count partipants who are taking part
      right_join(contactdata %>%
                   filter(!is.na(cnt_age_exact)),relationship = "many-to-many",by = join_by(part_id)) %>% 
      mutate(agegp2 = ifelse(cnt_age_exact<=agebandsize,floor(cnt_age_exact/agebandsize),floor(70/agebandsize))) %>%#create age categories for contacts (make sure same as participant)
      group_by(agegp1,agegp2) %>%
      summarise(popsize=max(n),totalcontacts = n(),.groups = "drop_last") %>%
      mutate(contactrate = totalcontacts/popsize) %>%
      mutate(contactrate = ifelse(is.na(contactrate),0,contactrate)) %>% 
      pivot_wider(id_cols=agegp1,names_from=agegp2,values_from = contactrate, values_fill = 0) %>%   #turn into matrix
      replace_na(list(Value = 0))-> contactmatrix

    L1 <- ncol(contactmatrix)
    round(max(Re(eigen(contactmatrix[1:(L1 - 1), 2:(L1)])$values)), 3)
}

# Build a tidy grid of every (sample size × rep) combination and map the
# function over it. Each row is one independent draw; randomness comes from
# rbinom inside compute_eigenval, so the rep column is just a counter.
maxeigenval_1y <- crossing(ss = x2, rep = seq_len(numreps)) %>%
  mutate(maxeigen = future_map_dbl(ss, compute_eigenval_withreplacement,
                                   .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE),
                                   .progress = TRUE))

#nconst=compute_eigenval_withreplacement(7000)
```


```{r}

#norm_const <- mean(maxeigenval[length(x), ])
norm_const <- as.numeric(maxeigenval_1y %>% filter(ss==max(ss)) %>% summarise(mean(maxeigen)))
#norm_const <-as.numeric(nconst)
#maxeigenval <- maxeigenval / norm_const
maxeigenval_1y <- maxeigenval_1y %>% mutate(maxeigen_norm = maxeigen / norm_const)
```

```{r}
#save(maxeigenval,file="maxeigenval.RData")
save(maxeigenval_1y,file="maxeigenval_1y.RData") 
```


```{r}
# Scatter of normalised dominant eigenvalues across all reps, by sample size

maxeigenval_1y %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ss, y = maxeigen_norm)) +
    geom_point(size = 1.2, alpha = 0.3, colour = 'darkblue') +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1,   colour = "black") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 2,   colour = "darkgrey", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, colour = "darkgrey", linetype = "dashed") +
    xlab("Sample size") +
    ylab(expression("Normalised " * R)) + 
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text  = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14)
    )-> paral1

paral1

ggsave("PM1.png", paral1, height = H, width = H * goldenratio,
       dpi = 600, device = "png")
```




```{r polymod-simulation-20y}
#| cache: true   # simulation is slow; cache to avoid re-running on re-render

# Extract the per-draw computation into a plain function.
# Takes a sample size ss, runs the full sub-sampling pipeline once,
# and returns the scalar dominant eigenvalue of the contact matrix.

agebandsize=20

compute_eigenval_withreplacement <- function(ss) {
    ix = data.frame(part_id=sample(participantdata$part_id,size=ss,replace=TRUE))
    participantdata %>% 
      right_join(ix,multiple="all",unmatched="drop",by = join_by(part_id)) %>% #decide whether this participant is included in the sample, could be sampled more than once
      mutate(agegp1 = ifelse(part_age<=70,floor(part_age/agebandsize), floor(70/agebandsize))) %>%            #create age categories, in this case 10-year age bands
      add_count(agegp1) %>%  ##count partipants who are taking part
      right_join(contactdata %>%
                   filter(!is.na(cnt_age_exact)),relationship = "many-to-many",by = join_by(part_id)) %>% 
      mutate(agegp2 = ifelse(cnt_age_exact<=agebandsize,floor(cnt_age_exact/agebandsize),floor(70/agebandsize))) %>%#create age categories for contacts (make sure same as participant)
      group_by(agegp1,agegp2) %>%
      summarise(popsize=max(n),totalcontacts = n(),.groups = "drop_last") %>%
      mutate(contactrate = totalcontacts/popsize) %>%
      mutate(contactrate = ifelse(is.na(contactrate),0,contactrate)) %>% 
      pivot_wider(id_cols=agegp1,names_from=agegp2,values_from = contactrate, values_fill = 0) %>%   #turn into matrix
      replace_na(list(Value = 0))-> contactmatrix

    L1 <- ncol(contactmatrix)
    round(max(Re(eigen(contactmatrix[1:(L1 - 1), 2:(L1)])$values)), 3)
}

# Build a tidy grid of every (sample size × rep) combination and map the
# function over it. Each row is one independent draw; randomness comes from
# rbinom inside compute_eigenval, so the rep column is just a counter.
maxeigenval_20y <- crossing(ss = x2, rep = seq_len(numreps)) %>%
  mutate(maxeigen = future_map_dbl(ss, compute_eigenval_withreplacement,
                                   .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE),
                                   .progress = TRUE))

#nconst=compute_eigenval_withreplacement(7000)
```


```{r}

#norm_const <- mean(maxeigenval[length(x), ])
norm_const <- as.numeric(maxeigenval_20y %>% filter(ss==max(ss)) %>% summarise(mean(maxeigen)))
#norm_const <-as.numeric(nconst)
#maxeigenval <- maxeigenval / norm_const
maxeigenval_20y <- maxeigenval_20y %>% mutate(maxeigen_norm = maxeigen / norm_const)
```

```{r}
#save(maxeigenval,file="maxeigenval.RData")
save(maxeigenval_20y,file="maxeigenval_20y.RData") 
```


```{r}
# Scatter of normalised dominant eigenvalues across all reps, by sample size

maxeigenval_20y %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ss, y = maxeigen_norm)) +
    geom_point(size = 1.2, alpha = 0.3, colour = 'darkblue') +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1,   colour = "black") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 2,   colour = "darkgrey", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, colour = "darkgrey", linetype = "dashed") +
    xlab("Sample size") +
    ylab(expression("Normalised " * R)) + 
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text  = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14)
    )-> paral20

paral20

ggsave("PM20.png", paral20, height = H, width = H * goldenratio,
       dpi = 600, device = "png")
```



```{r}
theme_big <- theme(
  text = element_text(size = 16),
  axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
  axis.text = element_text(size = 14),
  strip.text = element_text(size = 16),
  legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
  legend.text = element_text(size = 14)
)

((paral1 + ylim(0, 4.5) +
 paral5 + ylim(0, 4.5)) /
(paral10 + ylim(0, 4.5) +
 paral20 + ylim(0, 4.5))) &
  theme_big -> sensfig


sensfig

ggsave("sensfig.png", sensfig, height = H, width = H * goldenratio,
       dpi = 600, device = "png")

```



```{r comparison table}
library(knitr)
library(pander)
xtable=seq(200,5800,200)
ss5000 <- maxeigenval_v2%>%filter(ss==5800)%>%select(maxeigen_norm) %>% unlist() %>% as.numeric()
ss100 <- maxeigenval_v2%>%filter(ss==100)%>%select(maxeigen_norm) %>% unlist() %>% as.numeric()


maxeigenval_v2 %>%
  filter(ss%in%xtable) %>% 
  group_by(ss) %>%
  summarise(min1=min(maxeigen_norm),max1=max(maxeigen_norm),mean1=mean(maxeigen_norm),sd1=sqrt(var(maxeigen_norm)),
            ks=ks.test(x=maxeigen_norm,y=ss5000)$statistic,pval=ks.test(x=maxeigen_norm,y=ss5000)$p.value) %>% 
  mutate(diff1=lag(sd1) - sd1) %>%
  ungroup() -> table10


###1 year age bands 
ss5000 <- maxeigenval_1y%>%filter(ss==5800)%>%select(maxeigen_norm) %>% unlist() %>% as.numeric()
ss100 <- maxeigenval_1y%>%filter(ss==100)%>%select(maxeigen_norm) %>% unlist() %>% as.numeric()


maxeigenval_1y %>%
  filter(ss%in%xtable) %>% 
  group_by(ss) %>%
  summarise(min1=min(maxeigen_norm),max1=max(maxeigen_norm),mean1=mean(maxeigen_norm),sd1=sqrt(var(maxeigen_norm)),
            ks=ks.test(x=maxeigen_norm,y=ss5000)$statistic,pval=ks.test(x=maxeigen_norm,y=ss5000)$p.value) %>% 
  mutate(diff1=lag(sd1) - sd1) %>%
  ungroup() -> table1


###5 year age bands 
ss5000 <- maxeigenval_5y%>%filter(ss==5800)%>%select(maxeigen_norm) %>% unlist() %>% as.numeric()
ss100 <- maxeigenval_5y%>%filter(ss==100)%>%select(maxeigen_norm) %>% unlist() %>% as.numeric()


maxeigenval_5y %>%
  filter(ss%in%xtable) %>% 
  group_by(ss) %>%
  summarise(min1=min(maxeigen_norm),max1=max(maxeigen_norm),mean1=mean(maxeigen_norm),sd1=sqrt(var(maxeigen_norm)),
            ks=ks.test(x=maxeigen_norm,y=ss5000)$statistic,pval=ks.test(x=maxeigen_norm,y=ss5000)$p.value) %>% 
  mutate(diff1=lag(sd1) - sd1) %>%
  ungroup() -> table5


###1 year age bands 
ss5000 <- maxeigenval_20y%>%filter(ss==5800)%>%select(maxeigen_norm) %>% unlist() %>% as.numeric()
ss100 <- maxeigenval_20y%>%filter(ss==100)%>%select(maxeigen_norm) %>% unlist() %>% as.numeric()


maxeigenval_20y %>%
  filter(ss%in%xtable) %>% 
  group_by(ss) %>%
  summarise(min1=min(maxeigen_norm),max1=max(maxeigen_norm),mean1=mean(maxeigen_norm),sd1=sqrt(var(maxeigen_norm)),
            ks=ks.test(x=maxeigen_norm,y=ss5000)$statistic,pval=ks.test(x=maxeigen_norm,y=ss5000)$p.value) %>% 
  mutate(diff1=lag(sd1) - sd1) %>%
  ungroup() -> table20



table1 %>%
  left_join(table5,by=join_by(ss==ss)) %>%
  left_join(table10,by=join_by(ss==ss)) %>%
  left_join(table20,by=join_by(ss==ss)) %>%
  filter(ss %in% xtable) -> tableout

kable(tableout)

write.csv(tableout,file="table_sensitivity.csv")



```
